title: Octets, Byte Streams, Fragmentation, Buffering, and Partial I/O · Runtime Contract: Octet Text Boundary
summary: A parser assumes one Read returns a complete line, converts arbitrary bytes to text too early, loses buffered bytes, and treats a short read or write as end-of-message. Planned artifact: Add and verify the octet text boundary increment in fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.
Plan deterministic browser models, Go compilation, net.Pipe tests, loopback sockets, TLS, packet capture, proxy, fuzz, race, load, and production transfer gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Octet Text Boundary · Theory Octet Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 6262 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Octet Text Boundary contract for the supplied data.
